steps.title: Focus on the cause
steps.content: The student wants to identify what causes meanders. The relevant note says a meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of a riverbank and redeposits it on the opposite side.

steps.title: Reject extra details
steps.content: Choice A uses the Madeira River as an example but does not explain causation. Choice B describes how meanders change after forming. Choice C discusses high sinuosity instead of the cause of meanders. The remaining choice states the erosion-and-redepositing mechanism directly. The answer is D.
